name: Skillet Chicken Paella
recipeIngredient: Medium sweet red pepper - cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the skin from chicken breast. Rinse and pat dry with paper towels. Cut into bite-sized strips.
2. In a 10-inch skillet, cook chicken strips, half at a time, in hot oil for 2 to 3 minutes or until no longer pink. Remove chicken from skillet.
3. Add onion and garlic to the skillet; cook until tender but not brown. Turn off heat.
4. Add broth, uncooked rice, oregano, paprika, salt, pepper, and saffron or turmeric. Bring mixture to a boil.
5. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for about 15 minutes.
6. Coarsely chop stewed tomatoes and add with their juice to the pan. Add sweet red pepper strips and frozen peas. Cover and simmer for about 5 more minutes, or until rice is tender.
7. Stir in cooked chicken. Turn off heat and stir until heated through.
Makes 6 main-dish servings.
PER SERVING: calories - 297, fat - 6 g., cholesterol - 50 mg., protein - 24 g., carbohydrate - 36 g., fiber - 2 g., sodium - 642 mg. (To further lower sodium content, use low-sodium chicken broth, no salt added tomatoes and lite-salt.)
